issues.title: NEW: Copilot CLI 'all retries exhausted' with no specific error category in 4 runs today
issues.detail: Affected: Smoke Copilot (run 26594967220, gpt-5.3-codex), PR Sous Chef (runs 26595665041 + 26598471276, gpt-5-mini), Auto-Triage Issues (run 26596900064, gpt-5-mini). All 4 hit the same pattern: copilot-harness attempts 1-4 fail with exitCode=1, all category flags false (isCAPIError400=false, isMCPPolicyError=false, isModelNotSupportedError=false, isNullTypeToolCallError=false, isAuthError=false). Two of the 4 are gpt-5-mini (Sous Chef + Auto-Triage), one is gpt-5.3-codex (Smoke Copilot is on the alpha-model path that we already know is broken). The Smoke Copilot failure could be the gpt-5.3-codex routing problem masked behind copilot-CLI generic error. The 3 gpt-5-mini failures suggest a new model-specific reliability issue. Investigate by inspecting copilot-harness stdout/stderr captures (hasOutput=true in all cases — output was produced but final exit was 1).

issues.title: UNRESOLVED 5 DAYS: Avenger hits isMaxTurnsExit=true every run (--max-turns 25 too low)
issues.detail: 3 Avenger runs today (26596698302, 26599358074, 26602359656), all conclusion=failure with [claude-harness] attempt 1 failed: isMaxTurnsExit=true. The Claude CLI is invoked with --max-turns 25. Avenger is a CI-failure investigator that uses Bash to inspect failing logs — 25 turns is insufficient for the task scope (run 26596698302 already burned 16 turns just reaching the failure-detail grep). RECOMMENDATION ACTIVE FOR 5 DAYS UNACTIONED: either raise --max-turns in workflow frontmatter to ~50, or trim the Avenger prompt to converge faster. Today's 3 failures each opened a comment on issue #35532 (the cascade-tracking parent issue). Cumulative wasted cost across 5 days now exceeds $5.

issues.title: UNRESOLVED 9 DAYS: Changeset Generator + Smoke Copilot still routing to gpt-5-codex-alpha-2025-11-07 → 404
issues.detail: Run 26594967273 (Changeset Generator, codex CLI): 4 attempts × 5 retries each, all 404 'Model not found gpt-5-codex-alpha-2025-11-07' at proxy http://172.30.0.30:10000/responses. Workflow frontmatter still reads model: gpt-5.3-codex. Same proxy-routing bug we've flagged for 9 days. Smoke Copilot (run 26594967220) is ALSO on gpt-5.3-codex and failed in a related but masked way (see copilot-cli-all-retries-exhausted issue). Fix: ONE workflow-frontmatter edit in changeset-generator.md (and check whether Smoke Copilot should also move off gpt-5.3-codex).

issues.title: RECURRING with NEW VARIANT: safe-output target="*" without explicit item_number/pull_request_number — now appearing in 2 different tools
issues.detail: Contribution Check (run 26603360040): 1 add_comment failure 'Target is "*" but no item_number specified'. Smoke Claude (run 26594967360): 2 create_pull_request_review_comment failures 'Target is "*" but no pull_request_number specified'. Same root pattern (target=* config + agent fails to supply explicit number) but spreading to additional safe-output tool types. Yesterday's recommendation to validate at MCP boundary still relevant; the validation needs to cover all target=*-configured tools (add_comment, create_pull_request_review_comment, set_issue_field, add_labels, etc.).

issues.title: NEW: Smoke Codex set_issue_field failed 'No issue number available'
issues.detail: Run 26594967309 (Smoke Codex, gpt-5.4, agent step succeeded). The agent emitted set_issue_field but the workflow context did not provide an issue number to target. This is the same family of safe-output target-resolution failures as add_comment/create_pull_request_review_comment target=*. The set_issue_field tool needs item_number when there is no triggering issue/PR or when target config does not match the context. Either prompt-side guard (Smoke Codex must skip set_issue_field unless an item exists) or schema-side guard (require item_number when context cannot auto-resolve).

issues.title: Daily Safe Output Tool Optimizer cost trend: $6.05 → $9.90 → $11.33 (NEW HIGH, 87% rise in 3 days)
issues.detail: Run 26597857310 today cost $11.33, surpassing yesterday's $9.90 and Friday's $6.05. Workflow succeeded but token consumption keeps climbing. Likely causes: prompt size growth (e.g., loaded examples or analysis history), expanding scope, or model selection change. Worth investigating the prompt diff over the last 3 days to identify the growth source. At current trajectory this single workflow will exceed $15/day within a week.

issues.title: RESOLVED for this audit cycle: Smoke Claude Playwright FFmpeg-CDN timeout did not recur today
issues.detail: Today's Smoke Claude failure (run 26594967360) is a DIFFERENT safe-output target-star issue, not the Playwright install timeout. No firewall blocks on azureedge.net hosts today. Could be the allowlist was patched, or the Smoke Claude test changed scope. Either way the original critical from yesterday is not recurring.

issues.title: Firewall block rate normalized: 17.2% today (was 24.9% yesterday)
issues.detail: 3770 total requests, 650 blocked (17.2%). Down from yesterday's 24.9% spike. Major change: Playwright/Smoke Claude blocks absent today. Smoke Pi pattern (high block rate on internal api-proxy ports) likely continues but is by-design for that smoke test. Watch entry retained for trend tracking.

issues.title: NEW: PR Sous Chef gpt-5-mini failed 2 of 4 runs today (50% fail rate) — was 100% success yesterday
issues.detail: Today: 4 PR Sous Chef runs, 2 success and 2 failure (runs 26595665041, 26598471276) — both gpt-5-mini, both via copilot CLI, both hit 'all retries exhausted' with no specific error category. Yesterday all 3 PR Sous Chef runs succeeded. Could indicate (a) gpt-5-mini reliability regression today, (b) input/PR-content edge case, or (c) gateway flakiness affecting copilot CLI traffic. Related to the broader copilot-cli-all-retries-exhausted-no-category issue above.

issues.title: NEW: Auto-Triage Issues failed (gpt-5-mini, copilot CLI all retries exhausted)
issues.detail: Run 26596900064. Same generic copilot-CLI failure pattern as PR Sous Chef. gpt-5-mini may be the common factor.

issues.title: Day 5 confirmed: 41 copilot runs today, 0 ENOENT, 0 anthropic-beta errors
issues.detail: Copilot CLI 1.0.52 remains stable for the known failure modes. The new 'all retries exhausted no category' pattern is distinct — it produces output then exits 1, vs ENOENT which was a missing-binary issue. 1.0.52 still recommended as min.

issues.title: PR Sous Chef variance widened slightly today due to 2 failures (cannot compare turn counts on failed runs)
issues.detail: 4 runs today, only 2 succeeded — too small a successful sample to evaluate turn variance reliably. Held in watch state.
